purpose: Provides a refined framework for classifying emerging markets under deglobalization, replacing the binary aligned/non-aligned model with actionable portfolio tiers
components: Tier 1 — Strategic beneficiaries (India, Vietnam, Poland): receive FDI inflows and favorable trade terms
Tier 2 — Trade diversion conduits (Malaysia, Thailand, Indonesia): absorb rerouted goods, vulnerable to transshipment crackdowns
Tier 3 — Crossfire economies (South Korea, Germany, Taiwan): deep linkages to both blocs, forced into costly hedging
Tier 4 — Excluded (Russia, potentially China): permanent investable universe shrinkage
current_assessment: Framework is materially improved over binary model. Vietnam may be Tier 2 (vulnerable to crackdowns) rather than Tier 1. Classification boundaries are fuzzy for some countries (Turkey, Saudi Arabia, Brazil) and can shift with political events.
limitations: Country classifications can shift with political events
Boundary cases (Turkey, Saudi Arabia, Brazil) are not clearly resolved
Does not capture within-tier variation or sector-level differences
Tier assignments for China may change under different US administration policies
